heading: Civil and criminal penalties
label: 5410 Civil and criminal penalties
notes: Amendments1980—Subsec. (a). substituted “manufactured home” for “mobile home”.Pub. L. 96–399
Effective DateSection effective upon the expiration of 180 days following , see , set out as a note under .Aug. 22, 1974section 627 of Pub. L. 93–383section 5401 of this title
amendments: Amendments1980—Subsec. (a). substituted “manufactured home” for “mobile home”.Pub. L. 96–399

children.label: a
children.text: Whoever violates any provision of , or any regulation or final order issued thereunder, shall be liable to the United States for a civil penalty of not to exceed $1,000 for each such violation. Each violation of a provision of , or any regulation or order issued thereunder shall constitute, a separate violation with respect to each manufactured home or with respect to each failure or refusal to allow or perform an act required thereby, except that the maximum civil penalty may not exceed $1,000,000 for any related series of violations occurring within one year from the date of the first violation.section 5409 of this titlesection 5409 of this title

children.label: b
children.text: An individual or a director, officer, or agent of a corporation who knowingly and willfully violates in a manner which threatens the health or safety of any purchaser shall be fined not more than $1,000 or imprisoned not more than one year, or both.section 5409 of this title
